steps.prompt: Which words are the imperative verbs?

hints.Vos, Quirites: 'Vos' is a pronoun and 'Quirites' is vocative — neither is a verb
hints.deos, immortales: 'deos immortales' is 'the immortal gods' in the accusative — not verbs
hints.Quirites, orate: 'Quirites' is vocative, not a verb — but 'orate' is indeed one of the two imperatives

hints.to speak: 'to speak' is 'orare' in some senses, but here with 'deos' it means 'to pray/beseech'

hints.Indicative: Indicative would be 'oratis' — 'orate' is imperative plural

hints.orno, ornare, ornavi, ornatum: These are the parts of 'ornare' (to equip/adorn)
hints.ordino, ordinare, ordinavi, ordinatum: These are the parts of 'ordinare' (to arrange/order)
hints.orior, oriri, ortus sum: These are the parts of 'oriri' (to rise) — a deponent verb

hints.observo, observare, observavi, observatu: These are the parts of 'observare' (to observe/watch)
hints.consecro, consecrare, consecravi, consec: These are the parts of 'consecrare' (to consecrate)
hints.obtempero, obtemperare, obtemperavi, obt: These are the parts of 'obtemperare' (to obey)

hints.Nominative: Nominative plural would be 'dei/di' — 'deos' with -os ending is accusative plural
hints.Dative: Dative would be 'deis/dis' — 'deos' is accusative, object of 'orate'

hints.1st: 1st declension accusative plural ends in -as — 'deus, dei' is 2nd declension
hints.3rd: 3rd declension accusative plural ends in -es — 'deus, dei' is 2nd declension
hints.4th: 4th declension accusative plural ends in -us — 'deus, dei' is 2nd declension

steps.prompt: What is the effect of the paired synonyms 'orate atque obsecrate'?
steps.target: Rhetorical amplification — doubling of similar verbs intensifies the plea
choices: Rhetorical amplification — doubling of similar verbs intensifies the plea
Cicero is being redundant by accident

hints.They mean different things: Both mean 'pray/beseech' — the doubling is for emphasis, not distinction
hints.It is a legal formula: While paired verbs appear in legal language, here the effect is rhetorical intensification
hints.Cicero is being redundant by accident: Nothing in Cicero's polished oratory is accidental — paired synonyms are a deliberate rhetorical strategy

translation: You, Citizens, pray and beseech the immortal gods.
